channel.title: BUscience
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from BUscience, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Cosmic voids look empty but they may be tearing the universe apart
item.description: Cosmic voids may seem like the emptiest places in the universe, stripped of matter, radiation, and even dark matter. But they’re far from nothing. Eve...

item.title: This 2-pound dinosaur is rewriting what scientists know about evolution
item.description: A nearly complete dinosaur skeleton discovered in Patagonia is helping scientists crack the mystery of alvarezsaurs, a bizarre group of bird-like dino...

item.title: Stanford scientists say colorblindness may hide a deadly bladder cancer warning
item.description: Colorblindness may be doing more than making traffic lights confusing — it could also be hiding a life-threatening warning sign. Researchers analyzing...

item.title: Scientists may have discovered a brand-new mineral on Mars
item.description: Scientists studying Mars may have uncovered a brand-new mineral hidden in the planet’s ancient sulfate deposits. By combining laboratory experiments w...

item.title: Scientists finally solve the mystery of yeast’s tiny centromeres
item.description: Scientists have uncovered how brewer’s yeast developed its unusually tiny centromeres, the DNA regions that guide chromosome separation during cell di...

item.title: NSF-DOE Vera C. Rubin Observatory launches real-time discovery machine for monitoring the night sky
item.description: NSF-DOE Rubin Observatory has issued its first scientific alerts, enabling dynamic, real-time observation of the night sky. The alerts are expected to...

item.title: NSF to proceed with demolition and site restoration at Sacramento Peak Observatory
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation announced today it will proceed with demolition and site restoration at the Sacramento Peak Observatory, also kno...

item.title: NSF launches $100M National Quantum and Nanotechnology Research Infrastructure program
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation is investing up to $100 million to establish a nationwide network of open-access research facilities for quantum ...

item.title: NSF announces first AI-ENGAGE awards to modernize global agriculture
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation, in coordination with partner agencies from Australia, India and Japan, today announced the first cohort of award...

item.title: Transitioning NCAR-Wyoming Supercomputing Center operations
item.description: The U.S. National Science Foundation has informed the NSF National Center for Atmospheric Research (NCAR) that management and operations of the NCAR-W...
